issueReference.body:
## Is your feature request related to a problem?

delete_dir in python/src/utils.rs deletes objects one by one. For large directories on remote object stores this means unnecessary round trips and slow cleanup.

Refactor delete_dir to use ObjectStore::delete_stream or equivalent batched path.

Done when:
- delete_dir uses batched deletion
- Handles empty dirs, nested paths, deletion errors
- Existing Python filesystem tests pass

referenceImplementation.prTitle: feat: implement batched deletion in delete_dir function
referenceImplementation.prBody:
## Description
- Refactor `python/src/utils.rs` `delete_dir` to use `ObjectStore::delete_stream` for batched directory deletion on object stores, replacing per-object sequential `delete` calls.
- Keep behavior unchanged for success cases while preserving robustness in concurrent/nonexistent-object scenarios by tolerating `NotFound` in the delete stream.
- Add regression coverage in `python/tests/test_file_system_handler.py`

This reduces remote round-trips for large directory deletions and aligns Python filesystem behavior with existing batched deletion usage patterns in core.

issueReference.body:
### Feature Request

A new built-in reporter (--reporter=prompt) that generates the same structured AI prompt produced by the HTML reporter's "Copy prompt" button, but as .md files written to disk during the test run — making it accessible in CI pipelines without a browser.

### Motivation

The "Copy prompt" button in the HTML reporter is a great developer experience for local debugging, but it requires a human to open a browser, navigate to the failing test, and manually copy the prompt. This makes it unavailable in CI environments where test failures matter most.

issueReference.title: [docs] text-secondary CSS variable does not work with Tailwind v4
issueReference.body:
### Steps to reproduce

Steps:
1. Open this link to live example (from the official docs): https://play.tailwindcss.com/f1ZIr0qSNG
2. Inspect the computed text color of either body copy (light or dark mode) using `className="text-text-secondary"`.

### Current behavior

The computed color is a plain rgb (no alpha) color when `palette.text.secondary` includes alpha, making Tailwind classes like `text-text-secondary` resolve to the wrong opaque color.

### Expected behavior

The computed color should preserve the original theme token values including alpha channel.

referenceImplementation.prTitle: [docs] Use direct palette vars in Tailwind v4 snippet
referenceImplementation.prBody:
## Summary

Updates the Tailwind CSS v4 docs snippet to use direct Material UI palette CSS variables instead of `*Channel` tokens wrapped in `rgb(...)`.

## Why

Some palette values, such as `palette.text.secondary`, include alpha. Mapping them through `rgb(var(--mui-palette-...Channel))` makes them opaque, which causes classes like `text-text-secondary` to resolve to the wrong color.

This updates the snippet so Tailwind classes preserve the original theme token values.

Fixes #47870

referenceImplementation.prBody:
Fixes #11641 - Databricks identifier quoting issue with date partitioning.

When using "Validate by" with date columns on Databricks SQL datasources, SQL expectations with `{batch}` placeholder fail with:
```
[CAST_INVALID_INPUT] The value 'ReportingDate' of the type "STRING" cannot be cast to "DATE"
```

referenceImplementation.prTitle: fix(github): preserve trailing hyphens in repository names
referenceImplementation.prBody:
### Description:

Fix a bug where repository names ending with a hyphen (e.g., `my-repo-`) would have the trailing hyphen stripped when parsing the URL, causing 404 errors when trying to access the repository via the GitHub API.

**Root cause:** The `getRepoURLParts()` function reconstructed the URL via `url.URL.String()` and then re-parsed it by splitting on `/`. This process could lose trailing hyphens.

issueReference.title: transform.filter.code regexes with .+ and /s flag cause "Maximum call stack size exceeded" on large files
issueReference.body:
Hi! 👋 
      
Firstly, thanks for your work on this project! 🙂

Today I used patch-package to patch `vite@7.3.1` for the project I'm working on.

The package which had Maximum call stack size exceeded had a JS file 5.7MB...

Here is the diff that solved my problem:

```diff
-const workerImportMetaUrlRE = /new\s+(?:Worker|SharedWorker)\s*\(\s*new\s+URL.+?import\.meta\.url/s;
+const workerImportMetaUrlRE = /import\.meta\.url/;
```

referenceImplementation.prTitle: fix: use precise regexes for transform filter to avoid backtracking
referenceImplementation.prBody:
## What this PR solves

Fixes #21696

The `filter.code` regexes in `workerImportMetaUrl`, `assetImportMetaUrl`, and `rolldownDepPlugin` used `.+` (or `.+?`) with the `/s` flag. On large files (5MB+) containing `new URL(...)` without `import.meta.url`, this caused catastrophic backtracking — leading to "Maximum call stack size exceeded" or multi-second stalls.

### What caused the bug

The `/s` flag makes `.` match newlines, so `.+` in `/new\s+URL.+import\.meta\.url/s` consumes the entire remaining file after each `new URL` occurrence. When `import.meta.url` is absent, the regex engine backtrac

referenceImplementation.prTitle: [OneTimePasswordField] Fix iOS Chrome autocomplete
referenceImplementation.prBody:
Autocomplete in `OneTimePasswordField`s is broken on iOS Chrome.

This is because:
- `OneTimePasswordFieldInput` **expects** a password manager / autocomplete provider to enter the full code in the first input.
- **However**, iOS chrome autocomplete appears to limit the length of its `input` event's value for the `maxLength` property set on the target input

The solution is to increase the `maxLength` on the input that expects to receive autocomplete input so that it can receive up to `collection.size` characters of input, rather than just one.

Closes #3641.

issueReference.title: [esp_http_client]: HTTP headers-done event (IDFGH-15293)
issueReference.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?

When dealing with ND-JSON, currently the esp_http_client caller cannot determine when all headers have been sent. For many types of HTTP requests/responses, the first HTTP_EVENT_ON_DATA event works fine for detecting this, but for streaming responses like ND-JSON, the first HTTP_EVENT_ON_DATA may be delayed until the first event stream is returned (which may not happen).

### Describe the solution you'd like.

I'd like a new HTTP event, HTTP_EVENT_ON_HEADERS_DONE, which is triggered by the empty line parsing in `http_on_headers_complete()`, be

referenceImplementation.prTitle: feat(esp_http_client): Event to signal last header downloaded (IDFGH-15314)
referenceImplementation.prBody:
## Description

Add an event to indicate when the HTTP response is ready to be received.

When parsing ND-JSON streams, this indicates the point when the ND-JSON stream is considered open, which occurs just after the last HTTP_EVENT_ON_HEADER.

ND-JSON stream clients cannot rely on the first HTTP_EVENT_ON_DATA, since that is only triggered by an event, which are optional and may never be sent.

Closes #15952

issueReference.body:

When using the Python Extension in vscode the command palette allows to easily create Virtual Environments but they have a fixed folder name `.venv/`.

Currently the Info Plugin only respects folders for Virtual Environments named `venv/`. If you create a minimal reproduction example the created `zip` is less than a megabyte in size. issueReference.title: Cypress crashes on SIGINT
issueReference.body:
### Current behavior

I'm running cypress and a few other commands using foreman. After tests have run, I Ctrl+C to quit foreman. This used to work fine, but now I'm always receiving a crash report for cypress when exiting. The crash happens when no tests are run as well. This message now displays in the logs for cypress:

> We detected that the Chrome browser process closed unexpectedly.
We have failed the current spec and aborted the run.

referenceImplementation.prTitle: fix: exit correctly so that darwin does not generate crash report when ctrl+c
referenceImplementation.prBody:
- Closes #29228
- Closes #29232
- Closes #33367

### Additional details
The child (electron) process's `close`/`exit` handler used to call `await getError()` (which called `await getPlatformInfo()`). Doing that async work during teardown can cause process trees to exit out of order, and trigger OS crash reporting.

The stdin `'error'` handler used to `throw err` for unexpected errors. Throwing inside the spawn promise causes an unhandled exception during teardown.

issueReference.body:
### Motivation

The current GPSampler does not support constant liar and hence is not very effective for batched / asynchronous hyperparam suggestions.

### Description

Maybe also extend the constant_liar feature to the GPSampler?

In the book 'Bayesian Optimization' by Roman Garnett, page 255. The sequential simulation + constant liar strategy is mentioned, e.g. imputing value with avg(y_observed) and claimed to have good performance.

referenceImplementation.prTitle: Add constant liar strategy to `GPSampler`
referenceImplementation.prBody:
## Motivation
Fixes #6392.

This PR adds a constant liar strategy to GPSampler. While some trials are still RUNNING, the sampler assigns them pseudo objective values, which is the best value among completed trials, and includes them in the GP update. This helps batch optimization by reducing the likelihood of proposing the same (or very similar) parameters when multiple evaluations are pending.

At this stage, the feature is limited to single-objective, unconstrained optimization.

requestedChange: Fix incorrect NatSpec terminology in contracts/token/ERC20/extensions/ERC20FlashMint.sol where two virtual function comments use 'overloaded' instead of the correct Solidity term 'overridden'. The NatSpec for _flashFee and _flashFeeReceiver currently says each function 'can be overloaded', but overloading refers to multiple functions with the same name and different parameters, whereas these are virtual functions intended to be redefined by child contracts. Replace 'overloaded' with 'overridden' in both NatSpec comments to match the consistent terminology used throughout the rest of the codebase.

issueReference.title: NatSpec: "overloaded" should be "overridden" in ERC20FlashMint
issueReference.body:
In `ERC20FlashMint.sol`, the NatSpec for `_flashFee` (line 70) and `_flashFeeReceiver` (line 81) says the function "can be overloaded" when it should say "can be overridden."

In Solidity, overloading means multiple functions with the same name but different parameter types. Overriding means a child contract redefining a `virtual` function. Since both `_flashFee` and `_flashFeeReceiver` are `virtual` and meant to be overridden by inheritors, the correct term is "overridden."

The rest of the codebase uses "overridden" consistently (Governor.sol, ERC721.sol, Votes.sol, ERC1967Proxy.sol, etc.).
